comment.body: Hey friends; my take is that the tariff negotiations will not be as smooth as many are hoping for. The main problem, as I see it, is that the admin has either sidelined or fired most of the regional experts because they didn’t see the bottom line value of such skills upon assuming office. Blame DOGE for this. This is turning into a tremendous problem because it’s hard to negotiate complex trade deals without have a grasp of the market and foreign policy idiosyncrasies. We are seeing this a bit with Japan right now and it will become more pronounced with other non-allied countries and probably more so, with Russia. There are talks this weekend but I don’t expect them to yield many results since the US side has neglected to utilize any of the traditional foreign policy assets that would allow them to have some sort of leverage in negotiations. Instead, many of these policy makers are going into meeting with little, if any briefings, that give context, that would allow rhetoric US to have any semblance of an upper hand in negotiations. This is the result of cutting things without a deep understanding of what you are actually cutting.
